parts: Project: Penny should turn rough product ideas into five memory-grounded directions, then let me pick a direction mix and export a coding-agent prompt.
A strong product loop is rough idea, assumptions, thought map, exploration, challenge, Defend or Revise or Absorb, Learn, and a final Idea Map plus Challenge Brief.
I prefer small reversible builds with explicit provenance, visible memory counts, source-backed claims, and a compact UI.
Decision: Make Claim and ClaimVersion the stable truth model, keep Move append-only, and record every AI operation as a BrainRun.

title: Personal creativity and coding prompt preferences

messages.content: Goal: I want Create to feel like a controllable thinking instrument for creativity, not a prompt generator that hides why it chose an option.
The strongest idea cluster is memory-native creativity: directions should cite memories, show uncertainty, and keep source refs available in the option drawer.
Frustration: I hate when AI tools produce a polished wall of text with no implementation sequence, no acceptance tests, and no do-not-break list.

For coding prompts, include product goal, user intent, personal context used, selected option history, UX, frontend, backend, data model, privacy, verification, implementation, acceptance tests, and do-not-break constraints.
Avoid broad document ingestion and generic semantic memory until the seed to challenge to learn to artifact loop is undeniable.
